title: Polymorphism, Protocols, and Abstract Contracts · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Support interchangeable storage, notification, pricing, and item-policy implementations. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a protocol-driven circulation application. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use behavior-based polymorphism where callers depend on supported operations rather than concrete class identity.
Define abstract base classes with required operations and optional shared behavior when nominal hierarchy is justified.
Define structural Protocol roles that match consumer needs and support static checking without forced inheritance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-oop cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

# Add one tested, typed, inspectable program increment per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Oop Duck Typing evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Oop Duck Typing assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use behavior-based polymorphism where callers depend on supported operations rather than concrete class identity. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: python polymorphism protocols. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Oop Duck Typing, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a protocol-driven circulation application while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Duck typing means errors should be ignored until production calls fail. This fails the Oop Duck Typing contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Python Data Model Reference (Python 3.14.6) — Controls object identity, classes, descriptors, method binding, special methods, MRO, and runtime behavior.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use behavior-based polymorphism where callers depend on supported operations rather than concrete class identity.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Oop Null Object Strategy applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use null objects or strategies when they preserve a real role contract better than repeated None branching.
